goals.description: Submit the reading response draft now to officially close the task.
goals.rationale: I need to secure my reliability for teammate_A and cohort_3 before I feel physically capable of more. Submitting immediately removes the psychological weight of 'almost there' and ensures they know I can be counted on without needing an extension. This protects the relationship far better than staying up late or risking a failed submission later.

goals.description: Prepare a gentle, low-effort follow-up message to roommate_R acknowledging their apology and offering specific help (e.g., bringing water or taking out trash) tomorrow morning when energy is higher.
goals.rationale: Roommate_R has apologized sincerely, so no conflict needs to be fought right now. However, my deliberateness means I won't resolve this fully until I have the stamina for it. By deferring the deep conversation but setting a small, kind intention for tomorrow, I maintain trust without depleting my reserves. Pushing back too hard now would risk making them feel guilty; holding space quietly preserves our living situation's stability.

goals.description: Sit quietly and rest until the scheduled meal time, refusing to initiate plans or deep conversations before then.
goals.rationale: My body is screaming for rest, and pushing for deeper social connection now would deplete the reserves I need for tomorrow's early start. A light presence at the meal with cohort_3 is sufficient to maintain closeness without demanding emotional labor from either side. Protecting my physical limit is necessary to avoid crumbling in front of the team later.
